questions.question: List and explain the three main categories of infrastructure in Data Warehouse Technical Architecture.
keyPoints: Hardware and Operating Systems - physical equipment and system software
Database Software - handles data storage, retrieval, and management
Collection of Tools - ETL, reporting, and metadata management tools

questions.question: What is a Data Warehouse Appliance? Explain any two advantages of using it.
keyPoints: Pre-integrated hardware and software solution optimized for data warehousing
Advantages: Reduced integration complexity, optimized performance, faster deployment, simplified maintenance

questions.question: Differentiate between Scale-Up and Scale-Out architecture in data warehousing.
keyPoints: Scale-Up: Vertical scaling - adding resources (CPU, RAM) to existing machine
Scale-Out: Horizontal scaling - adding more machines/nodes to distribute workload
Scale-Out better for very large datasets, Scale-Up simpler for moderate growth

questions.question: Explain the purpose and types of Metadata in a Data Warehouse.
keyPoints: Metadata serves as the roadmap of the data warehouse
Types: Technical Metadata (schemas, ETL specs), Business Metadata (definitions), Process Metadata (lineage, history)
Enables users to understand and navigate the data warehouse

questions.question: What is the role of the Staging Area in Technical Architecture?
keyPoints: Temporary storage location before transformation and loading
Allows for error handling and data validation
Provides checkpoint before committing data to warehouse
Enables recovery from failed loads

questions.question: Explain the ETL process and its importance in Technical Architecture.
keyPoints: ETL stands for Extract, Transform, Load
Extract: Pull data from source systems
Transform: Clean, convert, and aggregate data
Load: Insert processed data into warehouse
Critical for data integration and quality

questions.question: Describe the factors to be considered when selecting database software for a data warehouse.
keyPoints: Scalability to handle growing data volumes
Parallel processing capability for complex queries
Query optimization for fast analytical performance
Support for large tables and partitioning
Integration with BI tools and existing infrastructure

questions.question: Draw and explain the complete Technical Architecture of a Data Warehouse, showing all major components and their interactions.
keyPoints: Diagram showing: Source Systems → ETL/Integration Layer → Staging Area → Data Warehouse Storage → Metadata Repository → BI/Analysis Tools
Explain data flow from sources through staging to warehouse
Show role of metadata at each stage
Include feedback loop for data refresh
Discuss how components integrate and depend on each other

questions.question: Explain the concept of Modular Architecture in data warehousing. How does it benefit organizations? Discuss with examples.
keyPoints: Modular architecture: Components can be independently selected, upgraded, or replaced
Benefits: Flexibility, scalability, cost-effectiveness, easier maintenance
Example: Upgrading storage without changing database software
Example: Adding new BI tools without modifying warehouse structure
Supports evolution of technology without complete system overhaul

questions.question: Compare Data Warehouse with Data Lake in terms of Technical Architecture. Explain when each should be used.
keyPoints: Warehouse: Structured data, schema-on-write, optimized for reporting
Lake: Raw/unstructured data, schema-on-read, for data science/exploration
Architecture differences: Storage format, processing approach, user types
Use Warehouse for structured BI/reporting
Use Lake for advanced analytics, ML, big data exploration

questions.question: Discuss the significance of Metadata in Technical Architecture. How does Metadata improve the usability and manageability of a data warehouse?
keyPoints: Metadata as the roadmap - provides context and understanding
Technical Metadata: Enables system development and maintenance
Business Metadata: Enables self-service and user understanding
Data Lineage: Tracks data origin and transformations
Data Quality: Helps identify and resolve data issues
Repository: Centralized metadata management
